title: Light Thai Kao Mun Gai
description: A light and flavorful Thai dish from the Isaan region, made with mixed grains and a variety of spices. This recipe is inspired by the traditional Kao Mun Gai, but is adapted to be a comforting and accessible version for everyday eating.

ingredients.name: mixed grains (such as brown rice, quinoa, and farro)
chicken broth
ingredients.notes: make sure it's vegan-friendly if needed

ingredients.name: lemongrass
ingredients.notes: use the white and light green parts only

ingredients.name: soy sauce
ingredients.notes: use a gluten-free version if needed

ingredients.name: fish sauce
ingredients.notes: omit if vegan or replace with a mushroom-based sauce

instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine the chicken broth, coconut milk, lemongrass, galangal, and shrimp paste. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the lemongrass and galangal from the saucepan and discard.
Add the soy sauce, fish sauce, and sugar to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Add the mixed grains to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Return the saucepan to high heat and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 15-20 minutes, or until the grains are cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
Season the mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the Kao Mun Gai hot, garnished with chopped cilantro, lime wedges, and chopped scallions if desired.
Note: This recipe makes 4-6 servings and can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 2 months.
To reheat, simply add a little water to the mixture and cook over low heat until warmed through.
